content:
# Water Quality

**Summary**: The chemical, physical, and biological characteristics of water.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[2009-zubair|Zubair, 2009]] evaluated water-quality in the context of ozone treatment.

- [[2009-al-khaldi|Al-Khaldi, 2009]] evaluated the impact of contaminants on water quality.

- [[2011-ibrahim|Ibrahim, 2011]] assessed water-quality by monitoring disinfection by-products (DBPs).

- [[2012-iwalewa|Iwalewa, 2012]] evaluated the water-quality by modeling TDS levels over a 20-year period.

- [[2013-muhyedeen|Muhyedeen, 2013]] addressed the issue of MTBE contamination in Saudi Arabian water resources.

- [[2012-attyah|Attyah, 2012]] evaluated the environmental risk of drilling additives based on their potential to affect local water systems.

- [[2014-abdallah|Abdallah, 2014]] compared the water quality before and after bioremediation against national and international regulations.

- [[2014-adebayo|Adebayo, 2014]] evaluated the impact of MTBE on water quality and its removal via fly ash adsorption.

- [[2015-musa|Musa, 2015]] evaluated the impact of heavy metals on water quality.

- [[2015-bello|Bello, 2015]] evaluated the impact of pH and salinity on the remediation of pollutants in water samples.

- [[2015-yusuf|Yusuf, 2015]] evaluated water-quality by measuring nutrients, chlorophyll a, and microbial indicators.

- [[2016-salawu|Salawu, 2016]] assessed water quality by measuring the concentration of mercury (II) in aqueous solutions.

- [[2016-sameh|Sameh, 2016]] assessed the impact of salinity on the treatment efficiency of Toluene in groundwater.

- [[2018-adeyemi|Adeyemi, 2018]] evaluated the impact of SDAC on the water quality by removing MTBE.

- [[2020-ghazwani|Ghazwani, 2020]] evaluated the effect of feed water conductivity on ammonia removal.

- [[2021-siddiq|Siddiq, 2021]] assessed the impact of arsenic on groundwater quality.

- [[2021-alkhowaildi|Alkhowaildi, 2021]] evaluated the impact of high TDS (70,000 ppm) and hardness (7,000 ppm) on fluid performance.

- [[2026-fatima|Fatima, 2026]] monitored the evolution of recharge water characteristics including major ions and trace elements.

## Scientific Context

- [[2009-zubair|Zubair, 2009]] Ensuring water is free from contaminants and by-products in a resource-scarce region.

- [[2009-al-khaldi|Al-Khaldi, 2009]] evaluated the impact of contaminants on water quality.

- [[2011-ibrahim|Ibrahim, 2011]] assessed water-quality by monitoring disinfection by-products (DBPs).

- [[2012-iwalewa|Iwalewa, 2012]] evaluated the water-quality by modeling TDS levels over a 20-year period.

- [[2013-muhyedeen|Muhyedeen, 2013]] addressed the issue of MTBE contamination in Saudi Arabian water resources.

- [[2012-attyah|Attyah, 2012]] evaluated the environmental risk of drilling additives based on their potential to affect local water systems.

- [[2014-abdallah|Abdallah, 2014]] compared the water quality before and after bioremediation against national and international regulations.

- [[2014-adebayo|Adebayo, 2014]] evaluated the impact of MTBE on water quality and its removal via fly ash adsorption.

- [[2015-musa|Musa, 2015]] evaluated the impact of heavy metals on water quality.

- [[2015-bello|Bello, 2015]] evaluated the impact of pH and salinity on the remediation of pollutants in water samples.

- [[2015-yusuf|Yusuf, 2015]] evaluated water-quality by measuring nutrients, chlorophyll a, and microbial indicators.

- [[2016-salawu|Salawu, 2016]] assessed water quality by measuring the concentration of mercury (II) in aqueous solutions.

- [[2016-sameh|Sameh, 2016]] assessed the impact of salinity on the treatment efficiency of Toluene in groundwater.

- [[2018-adeyemi|Adeyemi, 2018]] evaluated the impact of SDAC on the water quality by removing MTBE.

- [[2020-ghazwani|Ghazwani, 2020]] evaluated the effect of feed water conductivity on ammonia removal.

- [[2021-siddiq|Siddiq, 2021]] assessed the impact of arsenic on groundwater quality.

- [[2021-alkhowaildi|Alkhowaildi, 2021]] evaluated the impact of high TDS (70,000 ppm) and hardness (7,000 ppm) on fluid performance.

- [[2026-fatima|Fatima, 2026]] monitored the evolution of recharge water characteristics including major ions and trace elements.

## Measurement and Characterization Methods

- [[2009-zubair|Zubair, 2009]] Chemical analysis of samples using HPLC and IC.

## Key Findings Across Sources

- Focused on removing contaminants and by-products from limited water resources. ([[2009-zubair|Zubair, 2009, p. 120]])

- Removal of Arsenic (As) and Mercury (Hg) is essential to ensure safe drinking water. ([[2009-al-khaldi|Al-Khaldi, 2009, p. 14]])

- Saudi Arabian groundwater is high in bromide content, making the potential for forming bromate during treatment a significant concern for water quality. ([[2011-ibrahim|Ibrahim, 2011, p. 21]])

- The study found that salinity is the principal concern for groundwater quality in the area. ([[2012-iwalewa|Iwalewa, 2012, p. 16]])

- Not stated in source ([[2013-muhyedeen|Muhyedeen, 2013]])

- Bentonite can form a viscous gel in fresh water, which kills fish by inhibiting gill action. ([[2012-attyah|Attyah, 2012, p. 87]])

- Water quality is a critical concern in EDC accidents due to long-term impacts on human health. ([[2014-abdallah|Abdallah, 2014, p. 48]])

- MTBE is a significant contaminant that makes water undrinkable due to taste and odor. ([[2014-adebayo|Adebayo, 2014, p. 13]])

- Heavy metals like Ni, Co, Cd, and Cr were identified as significant pollutants affecting water quality. ([[2015-musa|Musa, 2015, p. 15]])

- Salinity (TDS) levels of 0, 1823, and 3645 mg/L were tested to determine their effect on plant performance. ([[2015-bello|Bello, 2015, p. 73]])

- Chlorophyll a concentrations were significantly high only in Dammam at levels indicative of poor water quality. ([[2015-yusuf|Yusuf, 2015, p. 14]])

- The determination of the detection limit of Hg (II) ions using unmodified AgNPs was performed to ensure selectivity. ([[2016-salawu|Salawu, 2016, p. 71]])

- Salinity levels from 1250 to 5000 ppm significantly impacted the removal efficiency of Toluene. ([[2016-sameh|Sameh, 2016, p. 17]])

- MTBE removal efficiency reached 70% under optimal conditions (pH 6, 150 rpm). ([[2018-adeyemi|Adeyemi, 2018, p. 12]])

- Increasing the chloride content (and thus conductivity) had the greatest impact on ammonia degradation. ([[2020-ghazwani|Ghazwani, 2020, p. 15]])

- Arsenic concentrations above acceptable limits in drinking water pose significant health risks including skin lesions and respiratory complications. ([[2021-siddiq|Siddiq, 2021, p. 23]])

- High salt content in produced water is known to hinder thickening behavior after adding polymeric gels. ([[2021-alkhowaildi|Alkhowaildi, 2021, p. 19]])

- Monitored parameters such as chloride, nitrate, and sulfate in TWW during the experiment. ([[2026-fatima|Fatima, 2026, p. 91]])
